name: sled closer hack squat
instructions: Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable weight and position yourself with your back against the pad.
Place your feet shoulder-width apart on the platform, toes slightly pointed outwards.
Grip the handles on the sides of the machine for stability.
Engage your core and slowly lower your body by bending your knees and hips, keeping your back straight.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to extend your legs and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Grip the handles on the sides of the machine for stability. Engage your core and slowly lower your body by bending your knees and hips, keeping your back straight. Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below. Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to extend your legs and return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
